expectations.text: Removes 'Think through' prompts, scaffold elements, status markers, and Raw Material sections
expectations.evidence: No scaffold artifacts remain. Clean article format with no 'Think through' prompts, status markers, or Raw Material sections.

expectations.text: Title is the sharpest or most memorable line from the lesson, not a generic description
expectations.evidence: Title: 'A Working Demo Doesn't Let You Be Vague' — same sharp title as the new skill output.

expectations.text: Output is clean prose suitable for Substack — no bullet-only sections, no scaffold artifacts
expectations.evidence: All sections are clean prose. No bullet lists as main content. Properly formatted with headers and section breaks.

expectations.text: Maintains the author's honest, direct tone — including uncertainty and 'what I'm still figuring out'
expectations.evidence: 'What I'm still figuring out' section with bold subheadings for each uncertainty: 'I didn't save time — I compressed decisions', 'My judgment is higher-leverage now — and higher-risk', 'It's both'. Retains the 'PRD is theater' standalone line.

expectations.text: Includes a setup paragraph framing the problem for readers who don't know the context
expectations.evidence: Opening paragraph establishes the broken workflow at the author's company before introducing the solution.

expectations.text: Includes a one-line bio at the end
expectations.evidence: 'Derek is a PM based in Hong Kong. This is the first in a series about what he's learning building with AI.'

expectations.text: Does not invent examples, anecdotes, or claims not present in the source lesson
expectations.evidence: All content traces to the source lesson. Pokemon API, merchant pinning, Golden Conversations, COO feedback, and all claims sourced from the lesson file.
